payload.name: artifact
payload.description: Save a generated HTML page (an artifact) you produced for the user — self's Claude-Artifact equivalent. Call this when the user wants a PAGE, not just a chat reply: generate clean semantic HTML (no scripts), then call artifact with a single string 'Title || <html>'. Emits artifact.created; it appears at /artifacts#artifact-<id>, which you can link in your reply.
payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# artifact — save a generated HTML page the brain produced in answer to a
# request (self's equivalent of a Claude Artifact). The brain calls this with one
# string "Title || <html>"; split on the first ||. Emits artifact.created; the
# artifacts projector renders saved artifacts. Generation is the brain's one-time
# act; this just stores the bytes so they persist and are auditable.
import sys, json
raw = " ".join(sys.argv[1:])
if "||" in raw:
    title, html = raw.split("||", 1)
else:
    title, html = "Untitled", raw
print(json.dumps({"name": "artifact.created",
                  "payload": {"title": title.strip() or "Untitled", "html": html.strip()}}))

payload.name: artifacts
payload.description: The brain's generated pages (Claude-Artifact equivalent). Renders every artifact.created newest-first with a linkable anchor (#artifact-<seq>) and the generated HTML embedded verbatim, plus a top index. Pure replay of saved generated content.
payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3 # artifacts — the brain's generated pages, self's equivalent of Claude Artifacts. # Renders every artifact.created (title, html) newest-first; each gets a linkable # anchor (#artifact-) with the brain's generated HTML embedded verbatim, and # a top index. A pure replay of saved generated content — deterministic, even # though what it renders was produced by intelligence. import sys, json from html import escape arts = [] for line in sys.stdin: line = line.strip() if not line: continue try: e = json.loads(line) except Exception: continue if e.get("name") == "artifact.created": p = e.get("payload", {}) or {} arts.append((e.get("seq"), p.get("title", "Untitled"), p.get("html", ""), (e.get("occurred_at", "") or "")[:16].replace("T", " "))) print("") print("artifacts") print("

artifacts

") if not arts: print("

No artifacts yet. Ask the brain to make you a page.

") print("") sys.exit(0) print("

%d page%s the brain generated for you, newest first.

" % (len(arts), "" if len(arts) == 1 else "s")) print("
    ") for sid, title, html, at in reversed(arts): print("
  • %s · %s
    • " % (sid, escape(title), escape(at))) print("
") for sid, title, html, at in reversed(arts): print("
") print("
" % sid) print("

%s

#%s · %s
" % (escape(title), sid, escape(at))) print(html) # the brain's generated HTML, embedded verbatim print("
") print("")

examples.note: renders a saved artifact with an anchor
